mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, invalidating the threshold signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of cases, breaking the interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the index of industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during tightening regimes, or returns remain statistically flat.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Market absorbs inflation prints without negative returns, with positive outcomes exceeding 50 percent of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergent labor signals fail to predict returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output that directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals show no statistical association with forward returns, with p-values exceeding 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a supply-side contraction that forces corporate inventory drawdowns and earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Consecutive declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, invalidating the contraction signal.
